mnemonics.title: THREE TYPES OF BUSES
mnemonics.content: DAC: DATA ADDRESS CONTROL - REMEMBER AS 'DONT ACT CARELESSLY' (Data, Address, Control)

mnemonics.title: BUS PERFORMANCE FORMULA
mnemonics.content: BW = W × f / 8 (Bandwidth = Width × frequency divided by 8 for bytes)

mnemonics.title: SYNCHRONOUS VS ASYNCHRONOUS
mnemonics.content: SYNCHRONOUS: SAME CLOCK - SIMPLE, FAST | ASYNCHRONOUS: HANDSHAKE - FLEXIBLE, COMPATIBLE

acronyms.usage: Standard for connecting external devices like keyboard, mouse, printers
Internal bus for connecting expansion cards in PCs
Point-to-point serial bus replacing PCI/AGP in modern computers
Early standard bus for IBM compatible computers
Allows devices to transfer data directly to memory without CPU intervention
Dedicated bus for graphics cards (now replaced by PCIe)
Connects CPU to main memory controller (replaced by DMI in modern systems)
Intel's link between CPU and I/O controller

visualTips.visualization: Think of bus width as NUMBER OF LANES ON A HIGHWAY. A 64-bit bus is like a 64-lane highway, allowing 64 cars (bits) to travel simultaneously compared to 8 cars on an 8-lane road.

Imagine a round-table meeting where people (devices) want to speak (use bus). Centralized is like a chairman decides who speaks. Distributed is like everyone negotiates. Daisy-chain is like going around the table in order.
Synchronous is like speaking in rhythm with a metronome - everyone knows when to talk. Asynchronous is like a conversation with 'over to you' handshakes - no fixed rhythm needed.
Wait states are like a traffic light turning red for the CPU while a slow truck (memory) finishes its work at a warehouse.

analogies.analogy: Think of a bus like a city's public transportation system. Just as buses carry passengers between destinations, computer buses carry data between different parts of a computer.
Bus arbitration is like air traffic control at an airport. When multiple planes (devices) want to use the runway (bus), the controller (arbiter) decides which plane lands first based on priority.

analogies.anal analogy: Bandwidth is like the width of a water pipe. A wider pipe (higher bandwidth) can carry more water (data) per second than a narrow pipe.

analogies.analogy: Point-to-point is like a telephone call between two people - dedicated connection. Shared bus is like a party line (old telephone system) where multiple people share one line.
Bus protocol is like the rules of the road - traffic signals, lane markings, and right-of-way rules that ensure orderly movement of vehicles (data).
